selected_title: Exploring internal stickiness: Impediments to the transfer of best practice within the firm
question: Where does “Exploring internal stickiness: Impediments to the transfer of best practice within the firm” support or contradict the causal account advanced by “Factors affecting knowledge transfer and absorptive capacity in multinational corporations”?

evidence_spans.text: Abstract The ability to transfer best practices internally is critical to a firm's ability to build competitive advantage through the appropriation of rents from scarce internal knowledge. Just as a firm's distinctive competencies might be difficult for other firms to imitate, its best practices could be difficult to imitate internally. Yet, little systematic attention has been paid to such internal stickiness. The author analyzes internal stickiness of knowledge transfer and tests the resulting model using canonical correlation analysis of a data set consisting of 271 observations of 122 best‐practice transfers in eight companies. learned_claims: The inspected source addresses Appropriation, Ambiguity through the evidence summarized in the preserved span.
The abstract supplies a direct evidence span of 137 words; interpretation remains bounded to that span.
